title: Should I Rebrand or Reposition? | Daniel Fox
meta_description: Most rebrands are an expensive way to avoid a sentence-level fix. The adjacent market usually needs a reframe, with the same identity kept intact. Run the cheap experiment first. Daniel Fox, fractional B2C CMO.

acceptedAnswer.text: Most rebrands are an expensive way to avoid a sentence-level fix. The adjacent market you are chasing usually needs a reframe that keeps the identity intact: the same product and the same proof, with different facts foregrounded and a different fear addressed. Run the cheap experiment first, one campaign and one landing page reframed only, before anyone commissions identity work. Daniel Fox is a fractional B2C CMO who runs that reframe before the spend.
